title: Quiz: Domain Adaptation
description: Test your understanding of domain adaptation techniques for QML

questions.question: What is covariate shift?

options.text: The label distribution changes between domains
The input distribution changes but P(y|x) stays the same

questions.explanation: Covariate shift occurs when the input distribution P(x) differs between source and target, but the conditional P(y|x) remains the same.

questions.question: What does MMD (Maximum Mean Discrepancy) measure?
questions.explanation: MMD measures the distance between two probability distributions by comparing their kernel mean embeddings.

questions.question: What is the goal of importance weighting in domain adaptation?

options.text: Reweight source samples to match target distribution

questions.explanation: Importance weighting assigns higher weights to source samples that are more similar to the target distribution, effectively matching distributions.

questions.question: In adversarial domain adaptation, the feature extractor tries to fool the domain discriminator.
questions.explanation: True. The feature extractor learns to produce domain-invariant features that the discriminator cannot use to distinguish source from target.

questions.question: What is a common cause of domain shift in quantum computing?

options.text: Noise differences between simulator and hardware

questions.explanation: A major source of domain shift in quantum computing is the noise present in real hardware that isn't modeled in noise-free simulators.

questions.question: What should domain-invariant features achieve?

options.text: Features that cannot distinguish between domains

questions.explanation: Domain-invariant features are representations that cannot be used to distinguish between source and target domains, enabling transfer.

questions.question: What is the 'transfer gap'?

options.text: Difference in performance between source and target domains

questions.explanation: Transfer gap is the difference in model performance between source and target domains; smaller gaps indicate better transfer.

questions.question: Which are valid domain adaptation strategies? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Valid strategies include MMD alignment, adversarial adaptation, and importance weighting. Ignoring source data defeats the purpose of transfer learning.
